name: drift-guard
description: PostToolUse(Write|Edit) advisory that prevents design-drift regression — when a design-defining surface is edited but MEMORY.md + CLAUDE.md/project.tape are not, the next session (which loads memory at startup, not the diff) regresses to the old design. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_drift_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Fires on any of: (1) SSOT file edit — path ends in `.tape` or `/CLAUDE.md`; (2) a new artifact under a `decisions/` folder; (3) an explicit `@design-change` marker in the post-write content. Skips the mirror targets themselves (`/memory/`, MEMORY.md) and the guard's own install dir. Emits non-blocking additionalContext nudging the model to mirror the change into the project auto-memory (memory file + MEMORY.md pointer), or — for a cross-project governance rule — to ask the user to `sidecar sign` commons.tape/project.tape. De-dupes once per (session, file) via a CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA marker. Advisory-only (per guards-narrow-scope). 0.1.1 — kills the self-trigger noise: (a) sentinel scan now limited to source-code extensions (`.hexa`/`.py`/`.sh`/`.swift`/...), so prose-heavy docs (marketplace.json/README/CHANGELOG/SKILL.md) that merely DOCUMENT the marker token no longer self-trigger on every edit; (b) the sidecar repo's own `/hooks/drift-guard/` tree is now skipped (in addition to CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT cache) since editing the guard source is a self-edit, not project drift. NO opt-out by design.
